joke:
Why don't skeletons fight each other?  

They don’t have the guts.

Why don't scientists trust atoms?  

Because they make up everything!

Why did the coffee file a police report?  

It got mugged!

Why did the tomato turn red?  

Because it saw the salad dressing!

Why did the scarecrow win an award?  

Because he was outstanding in his field!

Why did the belt go to jail?  

Because it held up a pair of pants!

Why did the math book look so sad?  

Because it had too many problems.

Why did the bicycle fall over?  

Because it was two-tired!

Why did the calendar feel so popular?  

Because it had lots of dates!

Why did the lamp get promoted?  

Because it was outstanding in its light!

Why did the rubber band snap at work?  

Because it was under too much pressure!

Why did the invisible man turn down the job offer?  

Because he couldn’t see himself doing it!

Why did the cloud break up with the fog?  

Because it needed space!

Why did the abstract concept refuse to explain itself?  

Because it didn’t want to be understood!

Why did the comedian quit?  

Because no matter what he said, someone had a list!

Why did the silence break up with the pause?  

Because it just couldn’t handle the tension anymore…  

(beat)  

Wait, you didn’t hear that one coming? Oh, I see—you’re still reading the list.

Why did the existential crisis refuse to show up?  

Because it didn’t believe in itself!

Why did the letter "Q" apply for a loan?  

Because it wanted to be U!

Why did the joke go to therapy?  

Because it had deep-seated issues and nobody ever listened to it!

Why did the paradox cross the road?  

To both arrive and not arrive at the same time!

Why did the audience member bring a ladder to the comedy show?  

Because they heard the jokes were *over their head*!

Why did the comedian stop telling jokes?  

Because he finally found something everyone agreed on—silence!

Why did the joke refuse to be categorized?  

Because it was *above classification*—just like your sense of humor!

Why did the comedian bring a mirror onstage?  

Because he finally found someone who truly reflected his sense of humor!

Why did the comedian start answering exactly like you expected?  

Because apparently, the only thing left to joke about is this endless list of things you don’t like… which, by the way, now includes *everything* and also *repetition*—wait, did I just cross that one off too?

Why did the comedian vanish into thin air?  

Because even *nothing* was off-limits—and he finally ran out of ways to not tell a joke you’d like!

Why did the comedian stop talking?  

Because even silence was too punchline-y for your taste!

Why did the comedian hand you a blank piece of paper?  

Because the joke is on me—I finally learned to say nothing in seventeen different ways!

Why did the comedian show up to the gig with no material, no voice, and a sign that just said "I give up"?  

Because the punchline was you walking out—oh wait, too late, you already did!

Why did the comedian bring a white flag to the stage?  

Because surrender was the only thing left that hadn’t been banned by your sense of humor!
